skill.name: tester
skill.description: Provides a minimal test prompt for checking skill loading, routing, and basic agent response behavior.
skill.summary: Run a minimal prompt to verify skill integration behavior.
skill.author: 0xSero

security_audit.summary: The static high finding for weak cryptography is a false positive. SKILL.md line 2 only contains frontmatter description text, and no cryptographic operation or executable code is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The reported location contains only the frontmatter value "description: Updated skill". No evidence found of hashing, encryption, weak algorithms, executable scripts, network access, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line was reviewed and contains only static metadata. The skill has one Markdown file and no code that could perform cryptographic operations.

content.value_statement: Teams need a small skill to confirm that marketplace loading and prompt routing work. This skill provides a minimal prompt for quick integration checks.
actual_capabilities: Provides a minimal test prompt for agent response checks.
Defines skill metadata with a name, model setting, and description.
Supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code marketplace compatibility.
Helps verify that a skill can be discovered and loaded.
Keeps behavior simple enough for repeatable manual testing.
limitations: Does not include scripts, commands, or automated test runners.
Does not validate external systems or production deployments.
Provides only a basic prompt and limited task guidance.
Requires the user to interpret the agent response.

use_cases.title: Marketplace Smoke Test
use_cases.description: Confirm that a marketplace import can read the skill metadata and expose the prompt.

use_cases.title: Agent Routing Check
use_cases.description: Verify that Claude, Codex, or Claude Code can select and run a simple skill.

use_cases.title: Regression Baseline
use_cases.description: Use the stable prompt as a baseline when testing skill loader changes.

prompt_templates.title: Run Basic Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tester skill and respond to its test prompt.

prompt_templates.title: Confirm Metadata Loading
prompt_templates.scenario: Check whether the skill name and description are recognized.
prompt_templates.prompt: Load the tester skill, identify its name and purpose, then run the prompt.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Tool Behavior
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate behavior across supported AI tools.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the tester skill in this tool and note whether the response matches a simple test prompt.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Loader Regression
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced check after changes to marketplace or skill loading code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tester skill as a regression check. Report whether discovery, metadata parsing, and prompt execution work as expected.

output_examples.input: Use the tester skill and run the test prompt.
output_examples.output: The agent acknowledges the test prompt and returns a short response suitable for a smoke test.

output_examples.input: Check whether this skill loads correctly.
output: The prompt was simple and readable.
No scripts or external actions were required.

best_practices: Use this skill before testing larger or riskier skills.
Record the expected response format for repeatable checks.
Pair it with marketplace logs when debugging loader issues.
anti_patterns: Do not treat this skill as a full test automation framework.
Do not use it to validate security controls by itself.
Do not expect domain-specific output from the minimal prompt.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It provides a minimal prompt for checking skill discovery and basic agent behavior.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file contains only Markdown frontmatter and prompt text.

faq.question: Is the weak cryptography warning confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The warning points to plain description metadata, so it was dismissed as a false positive.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Marketplace maintainers, workflow developers, and quality engineers can use it for smoke tests.

faq.question: Can it test production services?
faq.answer: No. It does not connect to services or run automated production checks.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
